MINI MINUTES: January 14, 2009, Judge Nancy C. Davis, reporter- Lauren Martinez, CSR#11332, clerk-Beth Wilber, Dept. 306
10:35am:
Gregory Brown, Esq. and Darrell Thompson, Esq. are present in court for the plaintiff; Robert E. White, Esq. and Seth Skootsky, Esq. are present in court for the defense. Counsel are present for motion in limine rulings.
A.) As to the issue of whether the case will proceed as a Court or Jury trial:
The Court states its ruling is based on Probate Codes that deal with the subject of Breach of Trust. The Court's ruling is governed by Probate Code sections 825 & 16421, that state probate matters have no right to a jury trial, therefore the case will proceed as a Court Trial.
B.) Wells Fargo in limine motion #1: Re consent agreement, is denied.
C.) The Affirmative defense in the limine motion, presented by Gregory Brown, Esq. for Durney was denied. (The Court was not sure of the in limine number.)

The Court and counsel argue, respectively, the Court's ruling determining that the matter will proceed as a Court trial. Court and counsel discuss whether Wells Fargo will seek a writ.
11:05am
Off the record. The Court suggests that counsel need to speak with their clients to determine if a writ will issue. The Court agrees to continue the matter to Jan-15-09, 9:30am, to allow counsel to confer with their clients.
11:10am
Back on record: The matter is continued per Court to above date and time for further pre-trial matters. Judge Davis informs counsel that should no writ issue the Court trial will tentatively start on Jan-21-09, if a writ issues the Court will need to determine a new trial date.
